title: The One Where Rachel Has A Baby (2)

jokes.text: Not bad. You know that feeling, trying to blow a Saint Bernard out your ass?
jokes.setup: Ross asking how Rachel is feeling during labor
jokes.punchline: Rachel's extremely graphic and absurd analogy for labor pain
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd imagery that's both shockingly graphic and unexpectedly specific. The Saint Bernard choice makes it funnier than generic 'elephant' comparisons. Perfect economy and memorable phrasing.

jokes.text: I didn't know you were pregnant. Who's the unwitting human whose essence you've stolen?
jokes.setup: Janice revealing she's pregnant
jokes.punchline: Chandler's darkly comic characterization of pregnancy as essence-stealing
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler - sophisticated vocabulary ('unwitting', 'essence') applied to crude concepts. Perfect character voice with genuine wit behind the sarcasm.

jokes.text: I'd have to say the talking gorilla. 'Cause at least I could explain to him that you're making me eat him.
jokes.setup: Joey's hypothetical choice between eating a Seeing Eye dog or talking gorilla
jokes.punchline: Monica's logical but absurd reasoning about communicating with her meal
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist logic - taking the premise seriously leads to hilariously dark reasoning. Good structure building on Joey's setup.

jokes.text: Oh. Somebody went to college.
jokes.setup: Monica giving a thoughtful answer to his hypothetical
jokes.punchline: Joey's condescending response to intelligence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey voice - his anti-intellectual stance delivered with characteristic simplicity. Not groundbreaking but well-executed character comedy.

jokes.text: Relax. It's not like we're forking.
jokes.setup: Guy being uncomfortable about spooning position
jokes.punchline: Wordplay on cutlery/sexual position terminology
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that extends the cutlery metaphor. Clean execution though not particularly original or character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, look at you making up crap for me.
jokes.setup: Ross giving sweet explanation about why baby doesn't want to come out
jokes.punchline: Rachel's blunt deflation of his romantic sentiment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's no-nonsense attitude cutting through Ross's sentimentality. Strong economy and timing.

jokes.text: Do something! Get me drugs... or you light a fire up in there and just smoke it out!
jokes.setup: Rachel demanding help with labor pain
jokes.punchline: Escalating from medical request to absurd 'smoke out' suggestion
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from reasonable to absurd. The 'smoke out' image is vivid and unexpected, showing Rachel's desperation through comedy.

jokes.text: Beat you, sucker! Oh, baby.
jokes.setup: Both women in labor, competitive about who delivers first
jokes.punchline: Janice's inappropriate competitive celebration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Janice - turning childbirth into competition. Good character integration but fairly predictable for her personality.

jokes.text: Oh. Okay, Mr. Percocet.
jokes.setup: Guy claiming to have seen TV doctor in his room
jokes.punchline: Nurse's dismissive assumption he's hallucinating from pain medication
jokes.explanation: Good economy and realistic hospital humor, but not particularly memorable or character-specific.

jokes.text: Fake? Excuse me? Hello?
jokes.setup: Charlie calling the doctor fake
jokes.punchline: Joey's wounded pride at his acting being questioned
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey moment - his actor's ego is wounded by being called 'fake.' Great character integration with his soap opera background.

jokes.text: She was married to a gay ice dancer.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to share personal info to help Ross
jokes.punchline: First item in Ross's bizarre relationship history
jokes.explanation: Good callback that sounds absurd to outsiders but is accurate show history. Works as standalone absurdist humor too.

jokes.text: She gave birth to her brother's triplets.
jokes.setup: Continuing list of personal info
jokes.punchline: Another bizarre-sounding but true fact about Rachel
jokes.explanation: Another callback that sounds insane but is show history. Building nice escalation with previous item.

jokes.text: Her twin sister used to do porn.
jokes.setup: Final item in the list
jokes.punchline: Most shocking revelation in the escalating series
jokes.explanation: Good escalation climax - saves the most shocking for last. Perfect economy and callback integration.

jokes.text: We're trying to dial down the crazy.
jokes.setup: Joey's 'helpful' list of personal information
jokes.punchline: Ross's dry observation that this isn't helping
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction. Good economy and timing, acknowledging the absurdity while moving the scene forward.

jokes.text: Me, neither!
jokes.setup: Ross and Charlie agreeing they don't usually feel instant connections
jokes.punchline: Joey enthusiastically agreeing despite not being part of their romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey obliviousness - inserting himself into an intimate moment. Great economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: Don't say 'Oh, my God!' What?!
jokes.setup: Doctor seeing something during delivery
jokes.punchline: Rachel's panicked reaction to the doctor's exclamation
jokes.explanation: Good panic comedy - realistic fear response that's both funny and relatable. Shows Rachel's character well under stress.

jokes.text: I thought she had two heads.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining baby is breech (buttocks first)
jokes.punchline: Ross's relief that it's not something worse
jokes.explanation: Good nervous father humor - his mind went to worst case scenario. Relatable anxiety comedy with nice economy.

jokes.text: I can't do it. Please, you do it for me!
jokes.setup: Rachel exhausted from pushing during delivery
jokes.punchline: Her impossible request for someone else to give birth for her
jokes.explanation: Perfect desperation humor - the complete impossibility of the request makes it funnier. Great character moment showing Rachel's exhaustion.

jokes.text: Oh, thanks for coming out of me.
jokes.setup: Rachel holding her newborn baby
jokes.punchline: Casual thanks for the major ordeal of birth
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement - treating the miracle of birth as a casual favor. Perfect economy and unexpectedly touching while still being funny.

jokes.text: Oh, no. Baby Girl Geller-Greene.
jokes.setup: Nurse calling baby 'Baby Girl Greene'
jokes.punchline: Ross insisting on including his name in hyphenated version
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross moment - his need to be included and acknowledged. Good character consistency callback to his controlling tendencies.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm so glad you guys got drunk and had sex.
jokes.setup: Monica admiring the baby
jokes.punchline: Blunt acknowledgment of how the baby was conceived
jokes.explanation: Good Monica bluntness - stating the obvious in her characteristic direct way. Nice callback to the conception story.

jokes.text: I mean, one minute she's inside you... and then 47 hours later, here she is.
jokes.setup: Monica marveling at childbirth
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of the extremely long labor time
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about the lengthy delivery. Not particularly memorable but fits Monica's analytical nature.

jokes.text: She looks so real. You know what I mean.
jokes.setup: Phoebe looking at the newborn
jokes.punchline: Treating a real baby like she might be fake, then acknowledging the oddness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe weirdness - her unique perspective on reality. The self-aware 'you know what I mean' makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, I could squeeze your little head. I won't.
jokes.setup: Phoebe cooing over the baby
jokes.punchline: Dark impulse followed by reassuring restraint
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark Phoebe humor - the violent impulse made funny by her immediate self-restraint. Perfect economy and character voice.

jokes.text: I don't see you weeping because you put your slippers on the wrong feet.
jokes.setup: Rachel defending her post-birth crying as normal
jokes.punchline: Revealing she's crying over trivial things like misplaced slippers
jokes.explanation: Good postpartum hormone comedy - the specificity of 'wrong feet' makes it funnier than generic crying. Relatable and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Wait, it's not gonna be Baby Girl? I thought that was so original.
jokes.setup: Discussion about naming the baby
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic surprise that 'Baby Girl' isn't the actual name
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler sarcasm - his trademark delivery style applied to the naming situation. Not groundbreaking but well-executed.

jokes.text: That's not her name. I'm sorry, she just doesn't feel like an Isabella.
jokes.setup: Ross announcing the baby's name as Isabella
jokes.punchline: Rachel immediately rejecting it based on the baby's 'vibe'
jokes.explanation: Good parenting comedy - the idea that a newborn has naming preferences. Captures the arbitrary nature of name decisions.

jokes.text: Oh, great! At least she sounds like a biblical whore.
jokes.setup: Suggesting 'Delilah' as alternative name
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt assessment of the biblical Delilah's reputation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica bluntness - her characteristic directness applied to biblical history. Great economy and memorable phrasing.

jokes.text: Oh, no, it's gonna be named after some snack or baked good, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Monica saying she's had names picked since age 14
jokes.punchline: Ross predicting food-related names based on Monica's personality
jokes.explanation: Great character callback - Ross knows his sister well enough to predict her naming preferences. Good setup for the Emma reveal.

jokes.text: See, I don't want it.
jokes.setup: Monica revealing the name 'Emma'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate dismissal after hearing it
jokes.explanation: Good timing and economy - Chandler's quick dismissal after the buildup. Perfect deadpan delivery implied.

jokes.text: Besides, nothing goes with Bing. So I'm screwed.
jokes.setup: Monica giving up the name Emma
jokes.punchline: Her practical assessment of the surname compatibility problem
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about the phonetic challenges of 'Bing' as a surname. Monica's practical nature applied to naming.

jokes.text: Aaron Lipman-Guralnick would like to say hello to his future bride.
jokes.setup: Janice introducing her newborn son
jokes.punchline: Immediately arranging a marriage between newborns with overly formal introduction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Janice boundary-crossing - her inappropriate over-involvement. The formal tone makes the absurd suggestion funnier.

jokes.text: Well, then he gets a divorce. It's Ross.
jokes.setup: Janice asking what happens when Ross remarries
jokes.punchline: Rachel's matter-of-fact reference to Ross's divorce pattern
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback with perfect economy - 'It's Ross' explains everything. Shows Rachel knows Ross well and accepts his pattern.

jokes.text: I'm lucky if my kid gets to spend a weekend with her father... and little Miss New Boobs.
jokes.setup: Janice sharing her bitter divorce experience
jokes.punchline: Her crude characterization of her ex's new partner
jokes.explanation: Good bitter divorce humor with Janice's characteristic crudeness. The 'Miss New Boobs' phrase is memorable and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Man, you see the kid on that nose?
jokes.setup: Looking at Janice's baby
jokes.punchline: Ross commenting on the baby's prominent nose
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about baby features. Not particularly memorable but realistic reaction to prominent family traits.

jokes.text: And so it begins.
jokes.setup: Ross forgetting to get sodas while talking to a nurse
jokes.punchline: Rachel's ominous prediction about Ross's attention being divided
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Janice's warning with perfect dramatic irony. The ominous tone makes a small incident feel significant.

jokes.text: Oh, what, are you kidding? She's gorgeous! It's all Rachel.
jokes.setup: Monica saying she can't decide who Emma looks like
jokes.punchline: Ross's immediate attribution of all good features to Rachel
jokes.explanation: Good Ross character moment - his diplomacy and lingering feelings for Rachel. Realistic new father behavior.

jokes.text: Or you might get everything you've wanted since you were 15.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining why he doesn't want to risk relationship with Rachel
jokes.punchline: Monica pointing out the potential upside with specific age reference
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Ross's high school crush with Monica as the knowing sister. The specific age makes it more impactful.

jokes.text: It is like a freak show up here.
jokes.setup: Joey seeing woman breastfeeding twins
jokes.punchline: His crude characterization of natural nursing behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey immaturity - treating normal biological functions as entertainment. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Please. He'll be with his real family. The twins and little Miss New Boobs.
jokes.setup: Rachel dismissing Ross's commitment
jokes.punchline: Rachel adopting Janice's crude phrase about future stepmother
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Janice's influence on Rachel's fears. The repetition works but isn't as funny the second time.

jokes.text: And if not, we got to do it on a bucket.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler discussing possibly starting their family
jokes.punchline: Monica's practical assessment of their romantic hospital encounter
jokes.explanation: Good Monica practicality - finding the positive in unsexy circumstances. The 'bucket' detail makes it memorable and realistic.
